questions.q: What does 'Wat is jouw beroep?' mean?
questions.explanation: 'beroep' = profession/job. 'jouw' = your (informal). You can also ask 'Wat doe jij voor werk?'

questions.q: How do you say 'I work at school'?
questions.explanation: 'op school' is the fixed expression. Hospitals use 'in': 'in het ziekenhuis'. Companies use 'bij': 'bij [bedrijf]'.
'met pensioen' = retired (literally 'with pension'). 'Ik ben werkloos' = I am unemployed.
'alleenstaand' = alone-standing = single. Literally describes someone 'standing alone'.

questions.q: 'Mijn vrouw is lerares' — what does the -es ending tell you?
questions.explanation: '-es' is a feminine suffix. 'lerares' = female teacher. 'leraar' = male teacher.
'gescheiden' = divorced. From 'scheiden' (to separate). Soft 'g' at the start.

questions.q: Which is correct for 'I work as an electrician'?
questions.explanation: 'Ik werk als [beroep]' = I work as a [profession]. You can also say 'Ik ben elektricien'.

questions.q: How do you ask about someone's partner's job?
questions.explanation: 'Wat doet jouw partner?' = 'What does your partner do?' Using 'doen' for profession questions is very natural.
